properties.remark: AN EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN JUST EAST OF OREGON AND TRACKED 7 MILES TO THE NORTHEAST, DISSIPATING JUST PRIOR TO REACHING STILLMAN VALLEY. PRIMARILY TREE AND POWER LINE DAMAGE ALONG THE PATH WITH SOME STRUCTURAL DAMAGE, MAINLY TO SOME OUTBUILDINGS AND BARN.

TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED A TORNADO LOOKING NORTH FROM I-80 AND RIDGE RD AND DAMAGE INDICATES THERE WAS A TORNADO. THIS BEGAN AROUND 2:53 P.M. AT 41.4535/-88.3269 (3 WNW OF MINOOKA) AND ENDED AT 41.4800/-88.2106 (2.5 S OF SHOREWOOD). DAMAGE INCLUDED BLOWING A CAR OVER ON I-80, TWO UTILITY POLES DOWNED, AND SEVERAL TREES DOWNED OR DAMAGED. THIS TORNADO STARTED IN GRUNDY COUNTY, CROSSED INTO KENDALL COUNTY, AND ENDED IN WILL COUNTY WITH A PATH LENGTH OF 6.3 MILES.

EF-1 TORNADO BEGAN AT APPROXIMATELY 430 PM CDT ON MAY 23RD NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF S. ROCKY HOLLOW RD. AND E. CANFIELD RD. (41.97, -89.24). PATH LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 0.3 MILES AND ESTIMATED WIDTH OF 100 YARDS. PEAK WINDS ESTIMATED BETWEEN 90 AND 100 MPH. OUTBUILDINGS AT TWO FARMSTEADS WERE SIGNIFICANTLY DAMAGED ALONG WITH SEVERAL TREES AND POWER POLES. THUNDERSTORM DOWNED LARGE TREE ON THE PROPERTY OF A MANUFACTURED HOME AND IMPALED A SMALL BRANCH FROM THIS INTO ONE OF THE WALLS. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A VERY BRIEF TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 75 MPH AND PATH LENGTH OF 1.5 MILES TOUCHED DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF PIERPONT AVE AND MONTAGUE ROAD AND CONTINUED NORTHEAST INTO THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF ROCKFORD. THE TORNADO ENDED NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF FERGUSON AND LOOMIS STREETS. ALL DAMAGE WAS TO TREES ALONG THE TORNADO PATH.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 100 MPH AND PATH LENGTH OF 9.2 TOUCHED DOWN JUST NORTH OF ROCKFORD COLLEGE AND TRAVELED NORTHEAST INTO CALEDONIA. SIGNIFICANT TREE DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH EF-1 SPEEDS OCCURED IN A LOCALIZED AREA NEAR ROCK VALLEY COLLEGE WITH SPORADIC DAMAGE THEREAFTER. THIS TORNADO WAS PRODUCED BY A SUPERCELL THUNDERSTORM WHICH HAD ALREADY PRODUCED A BRIEF TORNADO UPSTREAM.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE WITH A FEW TREES SNAPPED VERY NEAR THEIR BASE. SEVEN LARGE AND NEW UTILITY POLES WERE ALSO SNAPPED ABOUT 10 FEET UP FROM THEIR BASE THREE MILES NORTH OF SOMONAUK. THE DAMAGE WAS CONSISTENT WITH WINDS UP TO AROUND 90 MPH.

A BRIEF EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 100 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF 0.75 MILES, AND MAX WIDTH OF 150 YARDS IMPACTED THE NORTH SIDE OF OTTAWA DAMAGING ROOFING MATERIAL AND SHREDDING TREES ALONG ITS PATH. THE ROOFING MATERIAL WAS ORIGINALLY WELL-ANCHORED TO THE WALLS, AND A NEARBY SNAPPED POWER POLE SUGGESTED A PEAK INTENSITY OF 100 MPH WINDS. EYE WITNESS ACCOUNTS CONFIRM THE DAMAGE WAS PRODUCED BY A TORNADO BASED ON CLEARLY RISING AND SWIRLING MOTION BY MULTIPLE SUB-VORTICES IN A BROAD CIRCULATION.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH PEAK WINDS OF 105 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF 14.5 MILES, AND MAX WIDTH OF 250 YARDS STARTED ON THE SOUTH SIDE OF YORKVILLE DESTROYING A PERGOLA, TAKING SIDING OFF A HOUSE, AND THROWING A TREE ACROSS A ROAD. THE TORNADO THEN CONTINUED SOUTHEAST AND DAMAGED SEVERAL FARMSTEADS INCLUDING THROWING A SHED SEVERAL HUNDRED FEET INTO A FIELD AND SCOURING GRASS. SEVERAL POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED AT THEIR BASE, AS WELL. THE TORNADO CROSSED INTO WILL COUNTY SOUTH OF PLAINFIELD AND CONTINUED DAMAGING TREES UNTIL ENDING JUST WEST OF I-55 NEAR CANTON FARM ROAD. A BROAD AREA OF WIND GUSTS OF 70 TO 80 MPH THEN FOLLOWED INTO CREST HILL LEADING TO PROLIFIC TREE DAMAGE.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 1 NNE LOMBARD. THE SAME STORM WHICH PRODUCED THE BRIEF WHEATON TORNADO PRODUCED ANOTHER REGION OF EF-1 TORNADO DAMAGE IMMEDIATELY EAST OF I-355 JUST EAST OF LILACIA PARK. THIS TORNADO ALSO CONTINUED IN A NORTHEASTERLY DIRECTION, UPROOTING TREES--SOME SNAPPED AT THE TRUNK--AS WELL AS CAUSING SOME HOUSE DAMAGE INCLUDING A FEW WITH SUBSTANTIAL ROOF DAMAGE. THE WORST DAMAGE OCCURRED NEAR AND AROUND THE LOMBARD COMMON JUST SOUTH OF ST. CHARLES ROAD. DAMAGE EASED CONSIDERABLY ON THE NORTH SIDE OF THE CIRCULATION, WITH A MORE GRADUAL DROP OFF IN DAMAGE TO ITS SOUTH. THE TORNADO LIFTED JUST NORTHEAST OF THE JEFFERSON MIDDLE SCHOOL.

A TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN JUST EAST OF SOUTH HARLEM AVENUE IN OAK FOREST, THEN MOVED NEARLY DUE EAST ALONG 151ST STREET PRODUCING MAINLY MINOR TREE AND STRUCTURAL DAMAGE BEFORE LIFTING NEAR THE I-57/I-294 INTERCHANGE IN MIDLOTHIAN. THE WORST AND MOST CONCENTRATED DAMAGE THEN OCCURRED NEAR MIDLOTHIAN IN THE REGION BETWEEN CICERO AVENUE AND PULASKI ROAD. SEVERAL LARGE TREES AND POWER POLES WERE SNAPPED NEAR THEIR BASES IN THIS REGION, PRODUCING DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH WINDS OF UP TO 100 MPH. DAMAGE FELLED CONSISTENTLY IN A CONVERGENT PATTERN TOWARDS THE TRACK CENTERLINE. THE TORNADO DAMAGE THEN BEGAN TO TRANSITION TO A REGION OF ENHANCED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS NEAR THE I-57/294 INTERCHANGE WHERE THE DAMAGE BECAME INCREASINGLY ORIENTED IN AN EASTERLY DIRECTION. DOWNED UTILITY POLES AND SNAPPED TREES INDICATE WIND SPEEDS WERE LIKELY NEAR 90 MPH, WITH THE MOST NOTABLE DAMAGE CENTERED IN AND AROUND THE HARVEY AREA BOUNDED ON THE NORTH BY SIBLEY BOULEVARD AND THE SOUTH BY 159TH STREET. THIS HIGH DEGREE OF WIND DAMAGE PERSISTED EAST INTO THE SHABBONA WOODS FOREST PRESERVE BEFORE EASING TOWARDS CALUMET CITY.
ALSO HAD PEA SIZE HAIL.

NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED THAT A BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN VERY CLOSE TO THE COOK/WILL COUNTY LINE NEAR SHABBONA DRIVE. IT TRACKED NORTHEAST WHERE IT PRODUCED A LOCALIZED AREA OF FAIRLY WIDESPREAD TREE DAMAGE CONSISTENT WITH HIGH-END EF-0 WIND SPEEDS. THE WORST DAMAGE WAS CONFINED TO A SMALL AREA NEAR MARQUETTE AND SOMONAUK PARKS, ALTHOUGH VERY LITTLE STRUCTURAL DAMAGE WAS NOTED. THE TORNADO LIFTED OVER SCHUBERTS WOODS FOREST PRESERVE. SPORADIC STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE OCCURRED EAST TOWARDS STATE STREET.
LARGE TREE UPROOTED IN OAK LAWN. TIME ESTIMATED BY RADAR.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY CONFIRMED AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS OF 110 MPH, PATH LENGTH OF 3 MILES, AND MAX WIDTH OF 300 YARDS TOUCHED DOWN NEAR CRAWFORD AVE IN THE LINCOLNWOOD NEIGHBROHOOD OF CHICAGO AND PROGRESSED EAST NORTHEASTWARD THROUGH PHILLIP ROGERS PARK. THE TORNADO THEN MOVED THROUGH POTTAWATTOMIE PARK AND ROUGHLY ALONG W FARGO AVE BEFORE CROSSING ONTO LAKE MICHIGAN. THERE WERE NO CONFIRMED INJURIES OR FATALITIES.
EXTENSIVE DAMAGE TO TREES REPORTED IN THE AREA ALONG 149TH AND 150TH IN HARVEY, ESPECIALLY EAST OF DIXIE HWY. POSSIBLE TORNADO.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY DETERMINED A REGION OF ENHANCED STRAIGHT LINE WINDS OCCURRED IN AND AROUND THE HARVEY AREA. DOWNED UTILITY POLES AND SNAPPED TREES INDICATE WIND SPEEDS WERE LIKELY NEAR 90 MPH. THE WORST DAMAGE WAS BOUNDED ON THE NORTH BY SIBLEY BOULEVARD AND THE SOUTH BY 159TH STREET. THIS HIGH DEGREE OF WIND DAMAGE PERSISTED EAST INTO THE SHABBONA WOODS FOREST PRESERVE BEFORE EASING TOWARDS CALUMET CITY.
